product_name: de Buyer Mineral B Pro Carbon Steel
reviewer_verdict: The reviewer rates de Buyer Mineral B Pro carbon steel cookware 10/10 — one of only two 10/10 ratings across 180+ cookware lines audited — citing its uncoated, chemical-free construction (99% iron, 1% carbon) as a durable alternative to PTFE-coated pans like the T-Fal Easy Care line, which the reviewer rated 0/10 for an undifferentiated PTFE coating.

claims.claim: Has no coating to degrade, avoiding the microplastic-shedding failure mode documented in PTFE-coated pans (the reviewer cites a University of Newcastle 2022 study measuring roughly 9,100 microplastic particles released per cooking cycle from a single scratch in PTFE coating) — though this is evidence about a category risk being avoided by construction, not a direct environmental test of this product

people_supply_chain.gap_note: No labor or supply-chain sourcing information was given for this product beyond it being French-made and the brand being owned by Groupe SEB.

claims.claim: One of only two cookware lines (of 180+ rated) to earn a 10/10 chemistry-safety rating, based on manufacturer PTFE/PFOA-free disclosure for its uncoated 99% iron / 1% carbon construction
Rated above competing carbon steel brands (Lodge 7/10, OXO 6/10, IKEA VARDAGEN 6/10, Made In 8/10) on finish quality and long-term ownership reports from r/carbonsteel and r/Cooking communities
Requires initial seasoning, reacts with acidic foods until well-seasoned, and is not dishwasher-safe
At $45-$90 for a skillet the reviewer expects to last 30+ years, cost works out to under $3/year, which the reviewer contrasts with a $25 T-Fal pan replaced annually

Its PTFE/PFOA-free status rests on the manufacturer's own product disclosure rather than independent lab verification (unlike some other picks in the same guide, which cite third-party XRF or leach testing)

notable_signals: Sources every rating in the underlying guide to one of four evidence tiers (manufacturer disclosure, independent XRF/leach lab test, class-action settlement or regulatory filing, or direct manufacturer verification) and states which tier backs each product — a reusable rigor framework.
The guide publishes a public corrections log (5 corrections with original rating, corrected rating, and the source that triggered the change) — a transparency practice around the source's own errors.

Cites a specific peer-reviewed study (University of Newcastle 2022, Science of the Total Environment) quantifying microplastic release from degrading PTFE coatings — a concrete, checkable data point for the category risk this product avoids.

attribution.reviewer: Clarity Insight Advisory (Buy Better With Brian)
attribution.note: Assessment derived from this reviewer's published work; see review_url for their full analysis.
